data.param: {"callBackUrl":"https://ddinggok.com/api/kie-callback","customMode":false,"instrumental":false,"model":"V4","prompt":"rainy night in Seoul","style":"Korean lyrics, Korean vocal"}

sunoData.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Umbrella in my left hand
Plastic bag in my right
Take-out getting colder
Crosswalk counting down the light

Taxi doors and tired eyes
Sidewalk steam
Breath in the air
Everyone rushing somewhere
I just stand and stare

[Chorus]
Rainy night in Seoul
And I still feel alone
Crowded train
Empty phone
Nowhere feels like home
Rainy night in Seoul
Every street says your name
I keep walking in circles
Back to the start of the same

[Verse 2]
Neon on the puddles
Blurs you in the glass
Our photos in that café
Scroll
Lock the screen
Pass

Bus stop full of strangers
You’d laugh at my wet shoes
Hear your voice in the traffic
Then it fades like the news

[Chorus]
Rainy night in Seoul
And I still feel alone
Crowded train
Empty phone
Nowhere feels like home
Rainy night in Seoul
Every street says your name
I keep walking in circles
Back to the start of the same

[Bridge]
Maybe you’re two stops away
Or halfway gone by now
If I called
Would you pick up
Or just let it ring out?

[Chorus]
Rainy night in Seoul
And I still feel alone
Crowded train
Empty phone
Nowhere feels like home
Rainy night in Seoul
Every street says your name
I keep walking in circles
But you’re not there at the end of this way
sunoData.title: Rainy Night in Seoul
sunoData.tags: Moody alt-R&B ballad; soft electric piano and muted guitar over a slow, swung trap groove. Male vocals close and breathy in the verses, chorus opens with wider reverb and stacked harmonies. Subtle strings bloom in the bridge, kicks stay deep and warm, airy hi-hats skitter around the vocal. Intimate, late-night mix with gentle saturation and tape delay tails.
